Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_23-32-48_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: 3 runes + 3 moonstones return 137 G
Expected: 137 (60 + 60 = 120 base + 12 first-insurance + 5 fee = 137)
Current error: 170 vs 137
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_23-32-48_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Need to generalize block logic. Components are "rune" and "moonstone" (those with base premium 25). Group items by type, and for each group of exactly 3 components, use 60 instead of 75. Keep all prior tests green.

Suggested approach:
- Define COMPONENT_TYPES = new Set(["rune", "moonstone"])
- Group items by type
- For each group: if type is a component AND group.length === 3, contribute 60; else contribute group.length * BASE_PREMIUM_BY_TYPE[type]
- This replaces the special-case `if allRunes && length === 3` check

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text:
All 11 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Generalized block logic by grouping items by type. For each group, if the type is a component (rune/moonstone) AND the group has exactly 3 items, contribute 60 G; otherwise contribute `count * base_premium`.
**Result**: All tests now pass (11 passing, 44 todo)
**Approach**: This is minimal - it replaces the special-case `allRunes && length === 3` check with a general grouping that handles separate blocks per component type. The new "3 runes + 3 moonstones = 137 G" test passes (60 + 60 = 120 base + 12 first-insurance + 5 fee = 137), and all prior tests remain green (including "2 runes + 1 moonstone" which doesn't form any block of 3 alike).

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_23-32-48_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
